summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their first regular meeting of 2023, serving as the organizational meeting for the new year. Key actions included electing Bob Hauserman as Board Chairperson and Dan Hodges as Vice Chairperson, approving the 2023 meeting schedule, appropriating $100,000 for ongoing tornado-related expenses, and authorizing the Township Administrator to sign contracts related to three Brownfield assessment grants totaling $387,700. The board also discussed gateway signage improvements, phone system upgrades using ARP funds, and scheduled a special workshop for January 17th to discuss potential zoning code changes.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda – passed
Motion to approve payment of bills (Nov 29, 2022 – Jan 9, 2023) – passed
Bob Hauserman nominated and elected as Board of Trustees Chairperson for 2023 (Hauserman abstained)
Dan Hodges nominated and elected as Vice Chairperson for 2023 (Hodges abstained)
Motion to adopt 2023 Board of Trustees regular meeting schedule – passed
Motion to set zoning meetings at 6 PM on trustee meeting nights – passed
Motion to schedule 2023 July tax budget hearing for June 27, 2023 at 6 PM – passed
Motion to set 2023 standard mileage rate at $0.655 per business mile – passed
Motion to approve sale of surplus items on govdeals.com (Resolution R001-2023) – passed
Motion to authorize Township Administrator to enter agreements with UltraFiber, CBTS, and ATC for phone/internet/cable and use ARP funds for phone system upgrades – passed
Motion to appropriate $100,000 in general fund for tornado-related expenses – passed
Motion to schedule special workshop meeting for January 17, 2023 at 6 PM for zoning discussion – passed
Motion to accept $200 donation from Garrett Boyd to the fire department – passed
Motion to accept resignation of firefighter/paramedic Chris Miller effective 12-26-22 – passed
Motion to accept resignation of Lieutenant Doug England as full-time and appoint as part-time firefighter/paramedic – passed
Motion to table item O (Sergeant West travel to hostage negotiations training) – passed
Motion to set 2023 Zoning Commission members' compensation at $50/month (not to exceed $599) – passed
Motion to set 2023 Board of Zoning Appeals members' compensation at $50/month (not to exceed $599) – passed
Motion to authorize Township Administrator to sign Brownfield grant engineering contracts – passed
Motion to approve 2023 road mileage for Clermont County Engineer at 47.062 miles – passed
Motion to accept resignation of part-time service employee Don Atkins effective 12-22-22 – passed
Motion to adjourn into executive session per ORC 121.22 G1 – passed
Motion to adjourn regular meeting at 9:27 PM – passed
action_items: Administrator Rose and Director Meadows to research pricing for larger gateway signs (option A style) and present firm numbers/images at next meeting
Administrator Rose and Director Meadows to map out recommended locations for gateway signs
Director Meadows to get pricing on a large welcome sign (e.g., across from the Bowling Alley)

Director Meadows to contact vendor about parking lot light pole installation
Chief Myers to provide Station 18 drawings to construction manager for cost estimate (expected mid-February)
Director Meadows to research pricing for equipping trucks with liquid salt application systems
Administrator Rose to sign Brownfield assessment grant engineering contracts for three properties
Administrator Rose to set up special workshop meeting for January 17 at 6 PM and invite prosecutor's office and zoning commission members
Fiscal Officer Laura to set up separate bank account for Brownfield grant funds
Administrator Rose to follow up on state reimbursement for tornado expenses submitted Jan 2-3
Harry Holbrook to provide sign sizing/font statistics to Director Meadows and Administrator Rose
key_amounts: $9,285.50 – quote for 10 gateway sign poles (without actual signs)
$100,000 – appropriated in general fund for tornado-related expenses
$250,000 – exhausted insurance line for tornado 'Other' expenses
$387,700 – total of three Ohio Brownfield assessment grants awarded ($78,100 + $130,700 + $178,900)
$29 million – Goshen Township's share of projects submitted for Appalachia grant
$34,000 – cost of 10 replacement radios going out of service
$22,000 – MARCS grant covering portion of radio replacement costs
$11,400 – estimated out-of-pocket cost for radios after MARCS grant
$30,000 – budgeted ARP funds for phone system upgrades (expected to come in at about half)
$200 – donation from Garrett Boyd to fire department
$50/month – Zoning Commission and BZA member compensation (not to exceed $599 annually)
$0.655/mile – 2023 IRS mileage rate for personal vehicle use
$650 – registration fee for tabled Sergeant West training (tabled)
47.062 miles – 2023 road mileage for Goshen Township
notable_topics: Organizational meeting: election of 2023 Board Chairperson and Vice Chairperson
Gateway signage design selection and pricing discussion
Township branding improvements (signs, window stickers, building signage)
Parking lot lighting pole installation update
Tornado damage ongoing expenses and state reimbursement submission
Station 18 repair drawings and cost estimate timeline
Phone system upgrade using ARP funds
Brownfield assessment grants ($387,700) awarded from Ohio Department of Development for three properties
Appalachia grant application update ($29 million in township projects submitted)
MARCS grant for radio replacements saving approximately $22,000
Zoning code workshop scheduled for January 17 to discuss text/map changes and overlay districts
Personnel changes: resignations of Chris Miller, Lt. Doug England (staying part-time), and Don Atkins
Liquid salt application system research for service trucks
2023 meeting schedule and tax budget hearing date set
Executive session regarding public employee matters
notes: This was the annual organizational meeting for 2023. Lisa Allen was recognized for serving two years as chairperson through the tornado recovery period. The fire department pulled two people from a fire in the past month. The communication tower behind the building is now fully functional after cutover. The board discussed that the Brownfield grant awards going directly to the township rather than through the county is unusual. Executive session lasted from approximately 8:04 PM to 9:27 PM with no action taken afterward. Fiscal Officer reported 2022 books are being closed out and 2023 budget is operational in UAN with no lag time. A new subdivision brought in 61 permits at the end of the year.
